mechanism_narrative: A sustained downtrend in FEDFUNDS toward the zero lower bound signals aggressive monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations re-rate upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9.5%, unemployment is near a cyclical trough-formation zone historically associated with policy response and base-effect improvement, generating a mean-reversion rally in equities over the following quarter as markets price in eventual labor market recovery.
regime_hint: labor market trough / early recession recovery
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or the rank correlation between the UNRATE-triggered signal and forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS and INDPRO indicates a synchronized labor-and-production upturn confirming genuine economic reacceleration rather than noise, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because the joint signal reduces false positives inherent in either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS+INDPRO acceleration signal produces forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from the single-series signals (no incremental Rank IC improvement > 0.02) across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate-policy regime, a continued decline in HOUST despite near-zero FEDFUNDS signals that monetary easing is failing to stimulate interest-rate-sensitive housing demand, indicating deeper credit or balance-sheet impairment that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and HOUST declines for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy / post-crisis balance-sheet repair
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during ZIRP periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls below 0.5% (effective zero lower bound), abundant near-free liquidity produces a short-horizon momentum drift in equities as cash is deployed into risk assets within days of policy confirmation, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns conditional on FEDFUNDS < 0.5% show no significant positive mean (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in PAYEMS reflects incremental improvement in labor income generation that supports near-term consumer spending expectations, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following each release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating the release carries no short-horizon predictive signal.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth outpaces CPIAUCSL inflation, real retail sales are expanding, signaling genuine consumption strength rather than nominal price effects, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as corporate revenue expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation
failure_condition: The real-retail-sales-growth signal shows no incremental Rank IC improvement (> 0.02) over the RSAFS-only signal across a 3-year walk-forward test, or forward returns following positive real growth are not significantly higher (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a rebound in INDPRO from cyclical lows signals restarting industrial capacity utilization that leads corporate earnings recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as production gains translate into revenue and margin improvement.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following INDPRO rebounds in post-recession periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
